occurrences.line_text: | done | Repair the sixtieth cycle's timing-publication false negative | Codex | One cadence-eligible, already-authorized CoinGecko request returned HTTP 200 with 80 bounded rows in 1,488 ms and no retry; 30 genuine observations were retained in non-authoritative `radar_market_no_send_20260721t190846637595z_883d8365d03e`. Publication failed closed because independently rounded six-decimal timing medians differed by one microsecond, and exact rollback preserved cycle 59 as dashboard authority. Future summaries now derive the affine error range from one rounded source; historical v5/v6 accepts only the one-microsecond serialization residue, while two microseconds still fails. Full strict doctor now blocks every schema error, and status explicitly distinguishes `last_provider_request_succeeded=true` from the terminal `publication_failed` result. The broad gate also closed latent official-macro schema drift: global events stay in the unified calendar, asset-specific scheduled derivation requires a typed real token symbol, and exact context-only counts are publication-bound without reinterpreting legacy manifests. Campaign truth is coherent at 60 real cycles / 1,800 retained / 1,770 baseline-counted / 30 too-close observations; no failed terminal generation was published. Final host-level `make verify` passed the isolated 1,525-test compatibility runner, 3,848 pytest cases, and matched smokes. |
